concepts.finding: Biased random walk with two parameters: p (return parameter - likelihood of revisiting a node) and q (in-out parameter - BFS vs DFS exploration). p and q can be learned from labeled data. BFS captures structural equivalence, DFS captures homophily. Outperforms DeepWalk by 26.7% on multi-label classification and 12.6% on link prediction.
concepts.implication: If keyword-intent clustering requires capturing both lexical similarity (homophily - keywords co-occurring together) and functional similarity (structural equivalence - keywords playing similar roles), node2vec's tunable p and q parameters can balance these.
concepts.open_question: What is the optimal node2vec p/q tradeoff for keyword-intent clustering? Is BFS-like or DFS-like exploration more important?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: node2vec embeddings (with tuned p, q) will outperform DeepWalk at recovering known intent categories from keyword co-occurrence graphs.
